MIT LICENSE at repo root (Brandon Correa, 2026) + README license section; verified by host.
51-aircraft real capture committed-ready at test/resources/ with r_dst/r_dir redacted (verified 0 occurrences); all trap-table quirks confirmed or explicitly not-observed; no doc corrections needed.
Build surface + skeleton tree; all bb commands verified; CLAUDE.md corrected for test:cljs reality.

HTTP server implemented under src/clj/adsb/http/: reitit router with Malli coercion on /api/aircraft/:icao (garbage icao -> 400 in middleware, no keyword interning), schema-checked responses, /healthz with stubbed feeder-status, static serving of resources/public, and idempotent start!/stop! now booted by bb dev via the :dev alias. aircraft-detail reads an injected state-lookup defaulted to empty state (404s until adsb-nqf.2). bb test:clj and bb lint clean. Commit 3f1c071.

CLJS browser runner built and proven: shadow-cljs :test build + adsb.test-runner (registers tests via env/get-test-data, parks result on window), Playwright driver (script/run-browser-tests.mjs, self-serving on ephemeral port) mapping run to exit code; bb test:cljs runs the real headless-Chromium suite. Proof: clean suite exit 0 (17 passed); deliberate failing test exit 1; removal exit 0. RTL Reagent component + React event test and day8.re-frame.test app-db isolation test added. Docs corrected (runner registry step, r/flush spelling, React-18 async findBy, ephemeral-port driver) and CLAUDE.md line restored.
All doc files verified against reality; browser test runner proven.

App shell landed in commit f7a46a1: index.html + #app mount; core/init! boots re-frame (:app/initialize-db) and mounts a full-viewport MapLibre map under a placeholder header. Map init behind the fakeable adsb.map.maplibre seam (create!/destroy! protocol); no-token demo-tiles basemap for DEV (prod tiles = adsb-kh4.5) with attribution; privacy-safe fixed regional center [lon -82.0, lat 28.0] zoom 7, never the receiver. CLJS test (test/cljs/adsb/map/view_test.cljs) asserts center/zoom + token-free style and that the shell mounts + inits the map through the seam. Verified for real in headless Chromium via the now-landed adsb-4ga.8 runner: 8 tests / 17 assertions, 0 fail; lint clean; live-app smoke shows canvas + attribution, 0 page errors. bb dev serves the app on :8080 via shadow-cljs :dev-http.

Trust boundary landed in 734e46e. schema.cljc: raw-aircraft honoring reality (alt_baro number|"ground"|absent, nullable optionals, absent-stays-absent), domain aircraft schema, consolidated anchored case-insensitive icao-address (also fixes the unanchored routes.clj placeholder regex), and number-based plausibility limits as a separate layer. ingest/coerce.cljc: ->aircraft (keeps position-less aircraft, drops identity-less; trims callsigns, lower-cases hex, drops implausible FIELDS without clamping) and unkillable ->aircraft-batch (one bounded log line per reject, clj/cljs catch backstop). Comprehensive cljc tests incl. batch resilience with mangled/non-map entries, log-count + log-boundedness tests, and a whole-real-fixture test: 51/51 accepted, 39 positioned / 12 never-positioned retained, all domain-schema-valid. bb test:clj 103 assertions green, bb lint clean, browser suite 87 assertions green in headless Chromium. Never-positioned decision recorded on the bead and written into validation-boundaries.md + testing-standards cast.

Added .github/workflows/ci.yml: temurin 21 + setup-clojure (Clojure CLI + babashka) + Node 20, m2/gitlibs/npm/playwright caching, npm ci, playwright install chromium --with-deps, then bb lint + bb test on push to master and PR. CLJS suite runs in real headless Chromium via script/run-browser-tests.mjs (no jsdom). Workflow compiles the :app build before bb test because the JVM static-serving route test needs the gitignored resources/public/js/main.js. Verified locally against HEAD in a throwaway git worktree (bb lint clean; bb test = 9 clj/cljc + 17 cljs browser assertions, all pass once app compiled). actionlint 1.7.12 reports the YAML clean. NOT observed running on GitHub: I cannot push, so remote CI green is unproven. Commit 3c85e3b.

Built the six-member fixture cast in test/cljc/adsb/fixtures.cljc (raw + domain forms, domain derived through coerce/->aircraft) with tests in fixtures_test.cljc. bb test:clj and bb test:cljs green; both files lint clean. Commit 7e62287.

Implemented src/cljc/adsb/geo.cljc: pure bearing/distance(haversine, meters)/bounds + aircraft->feature and aircraft-picture->feature-collection GeoJSON conversion. Tests in test/cljc/adsb/geo_test.cljc pass on JVM and browser (geo-test: 9 tests/42 assertions; full browser suite 28/146 green). Lint clean for the two files. Committed 5e246da via explicit pathspec.

Delivered adsb.ingest.source (Source protocol open!/fetch!/close! + fn-source stub), adsb.ingest.config (pure validate-feeder-url, fails loudly naming ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL), adsb.ingest.ultrafeeder (http-kit + cheshire Source feeding ->aircraft-batch), and adsb.ingest.poll (~1 Hz loop, log-once-per-state-change, exponential backoff to 30s cap, never dies, feeder status via poll/status). bb test:clj green (52 tests / 216 assertions), bb lint clean. Committed ae678d1 (only my 8 files, pathspec). RESIDUAL: poller is not wired at boot — main.clj is owned by adsb-kh4.1 and untouched here; healthz (adsb-kbm.2) + state store/SSE (adsb-nqf.2) must construct the poller, hold it, and read poll/status. See bead comment for the exact status-atom shape and callback seam.

Pure merge-batch/age-out/stale?/positioned? in adsb.aircraft (seen-at-ms = capture - seen; position retained across position-less updates, other absent fields go absent; retained across missed polls, aged out at 300s silence). Thin atom shell adsb.state wired as the http server's default :state-lookup. cljc+clj tests against the cast; bb test and bb lint clean. Commit b496548.

bb build now produces a deployable artifact. Added src/clj/adsb/main.clj (production -main: reads PORT [default 8280] and ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL from env — URL captured, not yet used, for adsb-nqf.1 to validate; starts adsb.http.server; blocks), build.clj + :build alias (tools.build) that AOTs adsb.main and packages resources/ incl. the shadow release frontend into target/adsb.jar, and a bb build task that runs 'shadow-cljs release app' before cutting the jar. Verified end-to-end from a clean worktree at the commit (1afc7cf): bb build -> target/adsb.jar (~19MB, 0 warnings); java -jar on an ephemeral PORT -> /healthz 200 JSON, / serves index.html (302->/index.html->200), /js/main.js serves the optimized release bundle (1.14MB), unknown icao 404s, clean shutdown. bb test:clj (19 tests/108 assertions, incl. adsb.main-test) and bb lint (0/0) pass. RESIDUAL: the acceptance clause 'serves the live map against a reachable feeder' is NOT met — feeder ingest/aircraft-store wiring (adsb-nqf.1/nqf.2) is not yet connected into the running server, so the map has no live aircraft. Everything else in the acceptance criteria is met. Committed via pathspec in 1afc7cf; note deps.edn/bb.edn also carry a concurrent task's nrepl-alias edits, swept in by whole-file pathspec (preserved, not reverted).

Plausibility layer built and committed (3cd83da). Range gate: adsb.ingest.plausibility/gate-range drops aircraft strictly beyond a configurable horizon (default 400 km / ~216 nm); position-less aircraft pass; nil receiver position disables the gate. Receiver position: adsb.ingest.receiver/resolve-position! once at setup - ADSB_RECEIVER_LAT/LON override wins, else feeder receiver.json (shape verified against the live feeder), else nil + one warn log; coordinates never logged. Jump flagging: >1200 kt sustained implied speed sets :aircraft/position-suspect? true - flagged never dropped/clamped; recomputed per observation (consistent follow-up clears; position-less observation inherits flag with inherited position); composed into adsb.state/apply-batch!. Schema: :aircraft/position-suspect? optional boolean. Privacy tested: coerce carries no trace of r_dst/r_dir; pipeline yields only aircraft-namespaced keys; committed receiver fixture uses synthetic coords. Docs updated (validation-boundaries.md plausibility section + receiver-position sourcing + privacy). bb test (clj 70/287 + cljs browser 37/189) and bb lint clean. Boot wiring for gate-range left as a bead comment for the SSE/integration bead (main.clj owned elsewhere).

Delivered the SSE stream + boot integration. GET /api/stream: snapshot-then-updates at ~1 Hz over http-kit async channels, ': hb' heartbeats every 15s, client registry with on-close removal, drop-and-close slow-consumer policy (full-picture frames bound the backlog). Wire format is adsb.wire (cljc, bidirectional codec, allowlist projection): flat unqualified kebab-case JSON, envelope {at, aircraft[...]}; receiver position / r_dst / r_dir / rssi can never reach the wire by construction - privacy tests over the projection AND over serialized frames from the real fixture pipeline. adsb.main/start! is the one composition root (validate feeder URL loudly, resolve receiver position once, range-gate in the poll callback per nqf.3 handoff, age-out folded into the broadcast tick, live feeder status on /healthz); :dev alias boots adsb.main so bb dev streams. Required http-kit 2.9.0-beta1 (2.8.x never detects HTTP-streaming disconnects - own commit 0d3c7f8). bb test (83 clj tests/341 assertions + 41 cljs/210) and bb lint clean. Feature commit b0b8304, scoped pathspecs only. See bead comments for the full wire spec and decisions.

docs/design-questionnaire.md written and committed (d7fd06e): 15 async-answerable checkbox questions in 6 groups (mood/light, map's voice, density/chrome, motion, emergency loudness, typography/glanceability), each option tagged (N)/(S)/(T) where it echoes a candidate, closing with a how-answers-map-to-candidates note incl. likely hybrids. adsb-bvi.5 notes now reference it and wait on the Overseer's answers.

SSE client delivered. adsb.stream.source: EventSource seam (Connection protocol + connect!, redef-able like adsb.map.maplibre / adsb.ingest.source). adsb.stream: re-frame client — decodes each snapshot/update frame with the shared adsb.wire codec and WHOLESALE-REPLACES :aircraft/picture (icao -> namespaced domain aircraft; never a merge), and drives :stream/connection honestly: :reconnecting while EventSource auto-retries a CONNECTING drop, own exponential backoff (1s->30s cap, reset on open) when the source reaches CLOSED, :down surfaced past 3 attempts while still retrying — so a server restart heals with no page reload. Live EventSource lives in a private atom, not app-db; app-db holds only :aircraft/picture and :stream/connection (subs of the same names). Wired into adsb.core/init!. app-db contract for the map layer documented on adsb-2yu.4. Browser tests fake the seam (snapshot populates picture, update replaces wholesale, :live->:reconnecting->:down->:live lifecycle, backoff grows) under day8.re-frame.test isolation. bb test:cljs 45/228, bb test:clj 83/341, bb lint clean. Commit 2318c22. NOTE: src/cljc/adsb/wire.cljc shows an uncommitted reformat from a concurrent agent — left untouched, not in this commit.

Added adsb.ingest.replay: a fixture-backed Source (open!/fetch!/close!) replaying test/resources/aircraft-sample.json forever through an injected monotonic clock — positions dead-reckon along track at ground speed, seen ages accrue at half the clock so aircraft cross the 60s stale line without reaching the 300s age-out, per-lap reset keeps the picture full. Wired via ADSB_SOURCE=replay (no feeder URL required; ultrafeeder remains default and still fails loud on a missing/bad URL). Unit tests use a fake clock (advancing seen, moving positions, looping, determinism); config/main tests cover source selection. bb test:clj (93 tests, 363 assertions) and bb lint clean. Verified end-to-end: ADSB_SOURCE=replay clojure -M:dev -> /healthz feeder-status ok, SSE snapshot 39 positioned aircraft, later update 38 moved, some stale, zero false position-suspect flags. RESIDUAL: the acceptance 'bb dev renders a moving map' cannot be shown visually yet because the map aircraft layer (adsb-2yu.4) does not exist; the SSE-level proof of moving/aging aircraft is the honest equivalent today. Skipped bb test:cljs to avoid colliding with the concurrent src/cljs agent; my change is clj-only. Commit 0b403f4. Not pushed.

shadow-cljs.edn now carries :deps {:aliases [:cljs]}; CLJS deps + test paths moved to a :cljs alias in deps.edn. malli declared once. bb test:cljs green (45 tests / 228 assertions), bb test:clj green (93/363), bb lint clean.

Coercion surfaces :aircraft/mlat? true for MLAT-derived positions (type=mlat OR non-empty mlat array); true-or-absent marker consistent with on-ground?/position-suspect?; schema, wire allowlist (both directions), docstrings, fixtures comment, and tests updated. bb test:clj + bb lint clean; browser suite verified via force-spawn compile + run-browser-tests.mjs (52 tests, 265 assertions, 0 failures). Commit 7965e32.

All five children closed: Source protocol + poller, state store + staleness, plausibility (range gate + jump flagging), replay Source, MLAT confidence. Backend ingest verified live against the real feeder and feederless via replay.
Both children closed: reitit HTTP server with Malli coercion, SSE fan-out with privacy-allowlisted wire format + boot integration. Verified streaming real aircraft end-to-end.

Imperative aircraft layer landed, commit c7f2672. adsb.map.aircraft-layer: attach! waits on the map's load event, adds the GeoJSON source + a NEUTRAL circle layer through the grown maplibre seam (on-load!/add-source!/add-layer!/set-source-data! — clj->js at the very edge, so tests capture Clojure data), then starts reagent.core/track! over the :aircraft/picture subscription OUTSIDE any component: one picture change = one geo/aircraft-picture->feature-collection + one setData, zero React. Zero-re-render PROOF in adsb.map.aircraft-layer-test: shell mounted for real, 25 :stream/received frames dispatched — 25 setData calls (+1 load flush), map component render count pinned at 1, mount count 1, and unmount stops pushes. Ordering hazard: frames arriving before load land in app-db (wholesale-replaced per adsb-2yu.2), so app-db IS the latest-wins buffer; load flushes the current picture exactly once — tested (2 early frames -> 1 push, latest only). Clock: stale judged at js/Date.now at push time (frame arrival) via redef-able now-ms — the imperative edge owns the clock, the domain takes it as an argument. Feature properties icao/callsign/track/altitude(incl "ground")/emergency/stale all proven to reach the source, so adsb-2yu.5 is pure style-expression work on layer-spec. Browser suite 52/265, JVM 94/370, lint clean. NOTE: bb test:cljs currently fails to connect to the long-running shadow-cljs watch (SERVER INSTANCE OUT OF DATE after a concurrent deps.edn change) — suite was run via 'npx shadow-cljs compile test --force-spawn' + the Playwright driver; the dev watch needs a restart to pick up new deps, left untouched.

Symbol layer driven by MapLibre style expressions in new adsb.map.style ns (data). Icon: two SDF silhouettes (plane + dot for track-less) via seam add-image!, icon-rotate [get track], alignment map. Three-state altitude ramp (absent/ground/numeric) with has-guards; emergency overrides colour+size; stale fades opacity. Click contract dispatches [:aircraft/select icao] through the seam (event/UI owned by dgb.1). Tests + lint green (commit 39b7d1b).

Aircraft selection state + Reagent detail panel. re-frame events :aircraft/select (hard contract w/ 2yu.5) / :aircraft/clear-selection, app-db :aircraft/selected-icao, subs :aircraft/selected-icao + derived :aircraft/selected joined against :aircraft/picture. Panel renders callsign/icao/altitude/speed/track/squawk/vrate/seen-age with absent->em-dash (never 0), suspect+mlat badges, close button. Boundary-4 XSS-proof test passes: hostile callsign renders as escaped text, no img node. Selection survives updates, closes when aircraft leaves picture; dangling icao pruned by 1Hz UI-edge clock tick. 321 cljs + 370 clj assertions green, lint clean, app build clean. Commit bd77c2e.

bb.edn :init parses .env and passes it to the dev task's child processes via :extra-env. Real exported env wins over the file; missing .env is a clean no-op. Verified all three cases with a temporary probe task (since bb dev's ports were occupied). Caught two EDN-reader traps along the way: bb.edn allows neither #"regex" literals nor @deref.

Header (title, live total+positioned counts, UTC clock, connection chip), altitude+status legend overlay reading adsb.map.style constants directly (no drift), connection indicator honest across live/reconnecting/down. Wired into views/app-root. Browser tests green (counts render+update, three indicator states, legend swatches equal style constants); bb test + bb lint clean. Commit b6af0c7.

Aircraft list sidebar shipped (src/cljs/adsb/ui/sidebar.cljs + test). Reagent roster deriving from the same :aircraft/picture sub the map reads — one source of truth. Rows: callsign (icao fallback), three-state altitude (dash for absent, never 0), ground speed; badges for emergency squawk (adsb.geo/emergency?), position-suspect, MLAT. Delegated click dispatches existing [:aircraft/select icao]; selected row marked via :aircraft/selected-icao. Sorts: altitude high->low, callsign A->Z, seen-age freshest-first; absent values sort LAST in every mode (a fact the sky never reported cannot rank against ones it did). Filters: positioned-only and airborne-only (on-ground? true-or-absent semantics) as toggles; state under :ui/* keys with events+subs. ACCEPTANCE DEVIATION: distance sort dropped — it presumed a receiver reference, and the receiver position is PRIVATE by design and never reaches the browser; the only alternative (map-center distance) needs map-state plumbing outside this bead and isn't a true distance-from-antenna. Substituted seen-age sort. Full bb test (82 tests, 388 assertions) + bb lint clean. Commit ba33677 covers the two new files. Root mount in views.cljs and neutral CSS in app.css are applied on disk but left UNSTAGED: both entangle with a concurrent header/legend agent's uncommitted work; committing here would capture their WIP or reference not-yet-committed namespaces (adsb.ui.header/legend). Whoever owns views.cljs/app.css commits the mount.

Client-side staleness shipped: geo.cljc emits continuous :age-s (+ boolean :stale kept) and filters aged-out aircraft with adsb.aircraft/aged-out?; style.cljs interpolates opacity over :age-s between domain thresholds (in seconds); :mlat lifted onto features with a smaller-icon lower-confidence cue; a 5s client tick in attach!/detach! re-pushes the cached picture with a fresh now-ms so fades progress and silent aircraft vanish between frames/stalls. cljc + browser tests cover age property, age-out filtering, mlat, and the tick (fake clock). bb test + bb lint clean. Commit b2bfbbf.

All six children closed: app shell, SSE client, geo/GeoJSON, imperative setData layer (zero-re-render proven), data-driven styling, continuous staleness fade. Live aircraft render and age honestly in bb dev; the 1 Hz path never touches React.

Promoted emergency? into adsb.aircraft (squawk->emergency-kind -> :hijack/:radio-failure/:general; emergency-kind; emergency?); geo delegates its unchanged boolean feature property. Added :aircraft/emergencies sub and adsb.ui.alert ribbon (role=alert, under header, names callsign/squawk/meaning, click selects, stacks multiples, clears when calm). Panel + sidebar badges now name the meaning. Tests: aircraft_test emergency? (7500/7600/7700 + kinds + 0000/absent), alert_test (appears/names/click/clears/multiple), panel+sidebar badge meaning. bb test:clj 403 assertions, bb test:cljs 420 assertions, bb lint all clean. Commit 9c17abc.

adsb.env/read! backfills the process environment from .env at main's composition root. env->config was already pure and took the env map as an argument, so it was fed rather than changed. bb.edn reverted. Verified from a bare 'clojure -M' with bb absent: System/getenv nil, env/read! yields the .env value, an exported var overrides it. bb test:clj 116/460 green, bb test:cljs 479 green, bb lint clean.

Fading position trails shipped: pure adsb.trails cljc (capped per-aircraft ring, append-on-change, drop-on-leave) + second GeoJSON source/line layer with line-gradient fade, wired into attach!'s track/tick path. Trails vanish with their aircraft via live-icao filtering. cljc + browser tests green; lint clean. Commit 5383292.

Numbers-only session stats readout shipped (commit 0907293): server adsb.stats computes running max range from the private receiver position and message rate from a new ultrafeeder Metadata side-channel; wire envelope gains an allowlist-projected stats map (max-range-km + message-rate scalars only, no position/counts); client adsb.ui.stats renders/dashes them via a :stats/session sub. No receiver location anywhere in the browser. bb test (117 clj + 107 cljs) and bb lint clean.

Aircraft glide between 1 Hz updates via a rAF dead-reckoning loop: pure spherical destination math + project-aircraft in adsb.geo (cljc, unit-tested against the canonical worked example), integrated in the imperative layer as a third push path off the shared !state cache — aircraft source only, trails stay real, ~20 Hz push throttle, pauses on hidden tab, stops on detach, snaps to truth on real frames, holds gs/track-less and stale aircraft. Zero React involvement proven by a rAF zero-re-render test. Full bb test (581 assertions) + lint clean. Commit aa27786.

Browser-side enrichment against a static, backend-served sharded database (tar1090's own pattern); no SSE wire change, so the live path never waits and it degrades to absent when the DB is missing. adsb.enrich lazily fetches per-hex-prefix shards behind a redef-able seam, caches in app-db, and treats 404/network/malformed all as :absent (logged once); lookups are pure. bb db:fetch transforms tar1090-db's aircraft.csv.gz (Mictronics data, UNSTATED license — no LICENSE file, no stated terms) into resources/public/db/<abc>.json, gitignored and never committed. Panel shows Type/Registration/Operator rows (em-dash when unknown); sidebar shows the type code from already-cached shards only. Full bb test (117 clj + 119 cljs) + lint clean; db:fetch verified end-to-end (570,856 aircraft / 1,001 shards) and static serving + degradation proven. Commit 1a7ddb3.
All four children closed: fading trails, dead-reckoning interpolation (20Hz, visibility-aware), enrichment from tar1090-db shards, numbers-only stats readout. Polish complete.

Multi-stage Dockerfile (stage1: clojure:temurin-21-tools-deps-bookworm + Node20 + pinned babashka, cache-warmed from deps.edn/package.json/shadow-cljs.edn then bb build -> target/adsb.jar; stage2: eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine, non-root adsb user, jar-only), EXPOSE 8280, HEALTHCHECK via busybox wget on /healthz, env-only config. compose.yaml: app service (env incl. ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL tunnel placeholder, restart unless-stopped, port map) + documented commented cloudflared sidecar stub w/ TODO(adsb-kh4.3). .dockerignore + README Deployment section (build/run loop + env table). VERIFIED: bb build succeeds from clean (exit 0, 30MB jar); the jar run as the container would (java -jar, ADSB_SOURCE=replay) serves /healthz 200 {status ok,feeder-status ok}, / 302, and SSE snapshot+update frames. UNVERIFIED (honest residual): the Docker DAEMON is not running on this Mac (client 29.6.1 present, socket absent), so the image was NOT built/run and hadolint is not installed. Dockerfile validated by review only; first real docker build should confirm babashka install-script + NodeSource apt steps and busybox wget healthcheck on the target arch. Commit cbf7a74. Not pushed; concurrent-agent edits to deps.edn/main.clj/view.cljs etc. left unstaged.

Production basemap set to OpenFreeMap 'liberty' (https://tiles.openfreemap.org/styles/liberty): no-token, no-key, unlimited public instance, commercial use allowed — fair use permits one URL in dev and prod, so no browser-visible secret ships and security-checklist §3 stays true (no doc change needed). Attribution 'OpenFreeMap © OpenMapTiles Data from OpenStreetMap' carried by the source TileJSON, rendered automatically by MapLibre's attribution control. Verified: release main.js grep shows the OpenFreeMap URL baked in, demotiles gone, zero token/key strings; headless-Chromium render loaded the style + 15 vector tiles, attribution control reads 'MapLibre | OpenFreeMap © OpenMapTiles Data from OpenStreetMap', Florida basemap rendered. bb test 130 tests/581 assertions green, bb lint 0/0. Commit 2a56d76. Protomaps self-hosted PMTiles documented as the fallback if the public instance degrades. Variant (not provider) may be swapped by adsb-dgb.5.

Cloudflare Tunnel + Access service-token auth implemented and documented. config/feeder-auth-headers (both-or-neither boot validation, secret never logged) threaded config -> ultrafeeder + receiver fetches; compose sidecar stub replaced with home-side note + auth env placeholders; README runbook added. Tests: headers present/absent + secret-never-leaks-on-failure. bb test:clj (534) + bb test:cljs (618) + bb lint all clean. Commit a5faf7c. Operational tunnel/Access provisioning is go-live residual (adsb-kh4.6); no real tunnel created. main.clj env-threading edit left in working tree, riding with the Overseer's uncommitted dotenv work (not committed here).

Feeder health now rides every SSE frame: wire.cljc feeder->wire/wire->feeder allowlist (status+last-success only, error excluded), broadcast threads a :feeder thunk (main composes poll/status), client decodes to :feeder/status with a derived :feeder/health sub applying the unknowable rule (stream not :live -> :unknown), header shows a second feeder chip and relabels the stream chip's :down to 'Disconnected'. Full cljs suite (136 tests) + wire/broadcast clj tests + lint green; live-proof: real feeder -> feeder ok (36 aircraft), unreachable feeder -> feeder down while SSE stayed live, no error string on the wire. Committed 013610a.

TLS terminates at a Caddy sidecar (only published ports; app port unpublished); CSP/HSTS/nosniff/Referrer-Policy/Permissions-Policy verified on real responses through the proxy and the CSP verified violation-free in headless Chromium against the release build; concurrent-SSE cap (ADSB_SSE_MAX_CLIENTS=100) and per-IP cap (ADSB_SSE_MAX_PER_IP=4) enforced atomically in the stream registry with 503+Retry-After, proven with live curl connections at cap=2 incl. slot-freeing; XFF trusted only behind the proxy (rightmost entry) after finding http-kit poisons :remote-addr with leftmost XFF; receiver-position-as-secret and never-proxy-the-feeder recorded in security-checklist.md + validation-boundaries.md in the same commit (f843178). bb test + bb lint clean.

Direction settled and committed (292f84c). docs/design-direction.md records the verdict rendered from the answered questionnaire (a7dc144): THE SECTIONAL, DAY & NIGHT — Sectional base per Q1b/Q3c/Q4c/Q12c/Q15c, amended per the Overseer: (1) two printed editions following the system theme, night edition prototyped FIRST as the top risk; (2) Nocturne's motion inside Sectional's body — glides, always-on trails, easing chrome, soft pulses, but the emergency treatment never flashes; (3) quiet surface — glyphs only, callsign on hover, NO sidebar. Two commissioned inventions specced and gated behind prototype beads: the cast shadow (altitude as shadow offset/softness) and the Stack (live altitude ruler = list + legend + scale, vertical on desktop, horizontal on phone). Typography deferred to a bake-off bead against the real layout. Full day+night palettes with hex, basemap = recolored OpenFreeMap Liberty variants (provider from adsb-kh4.5), aircraft styling mapped onto src/cljs/adsb/map/style.cljs constants, emergency grave-but-unmissable (red pen circle, MAYDAY stamp, NOTAM strip, edge arrow, no camera hijack), receiver never shown, phone/desktop equal. archetypes/AUTEUR.md reference restored; overturned across-the-room glanceability bullet removed (Q15c). Ordered execution brief for adsb-dgb.5 in §11 — visual pass unblocked.
All children closed: build surface, real fixture, schemas/boundary, fixture cast, questionnaire, and the design direction settled: The Sectional, Day & Night (docs/design-direction.md).

The Stack is live and the sidebar is gone (commit 58ba374). adsb.ui.stack: a flight-level ruler (surface–FL450) on the right edge, lying along the bottom edge on phone — one DOM, --alt-pct mapped to bottom/left, neither stage degraded. The fill is built from adsb.map.style/palettes per edition, so the ruler IS the legend and cannot drift. Pure layout fns (altitude-pct, tick-band, stack-member?, ruler-gradient-css) unit-tested as arithmetic; airborne ticks at true proportional altitude with a sub-cadence CSS drift, ground cluster shelf, altitude-absent holding shelf (absent is never zero). Hover publishes :aircraft/hovered-icao (map-side highlight is a recorded follow-up), click fires the existing [:aircraft/select] contract, selected tick magenta+named, emergency tick red/hatched/always-named — nothing blinks. Verified visually against the replay fixture in headless Chromium: 44 ticks spanning 1.6–88.9%, approach stacking visible as overlapping ticks at FL330. Acceptance met: any aircraft findable and selectable with no list anywhere. bb test 150/742 green, bb lint clean.

Night edition PROVED in the running app (commit 43edbc3). Mechanism: adsb.map.theme (prefers-color-scheme seam, ratom, watch), adsb.map.basemap (pure palette-driven re-inking of the fetched Liberty JSON, keyed on source-layer+type; shields/pattern fills hidden at night, natural-earth raster hidden in both editions; OpenFreeMap's hosted dark/fiord examined and rejected — generic darks, not our artifact; sustainable path recorded in doc §3), adsb.map.style restructured into :day/:night palettes (single source of truth — legend follows via the theme ratom, halo = each edition's own paper, trail head opacity 0.5), adsb.map.view fetches the plate once and re-prints map+layer on flip, app.css custom-properties layer (rode in with the Stack commit 58ba374) themes the chrome. Verified against replay traffic in headless Chromium under BOTH emulated color schemes: night reads as a printed night chart, not an invert. One palette amendment proved: night roads at ~0.6 alpha (full-strength #6B5540 glowed like filament) — doc §2 table updated. bb test 555+742 assertions green, bb lint clean.

Security headers moved into the Ring stack (adsb.http.security), X-Forwarded-For hop count made configurable (ADSB_TRUSTED_PROXY_HOPS), JVM tuned for a 512MB container, .do/app.yaml added. Verified end-to-end against a live replay-mode process. SSE-through-DO-edge verification split out as its own P0.

Cast shadow PROVED and shipped behind style/shadows-enabled? (commit c6f5221). Technique: icon-translate is constant-only in MapLibre 4.7, so the offset is a per-feature property (adsb.geo/shadow-offset — pure, unit-tested cljc) consumed by data-driven icon-offset; because icon-offset rides the icon's rotated frame, geo counter-rotates the fixed NW-sun SE throw by each track — one sun over the whole chart, verified for arbitrary tracks in geo_test. Curve: sqrt(alt/40000) x 30 icon-px — half throw by 10,000 ft so the low sky's drama stays legible; ground/absent altitude cast NOTHING (property omitted, layer filtered on has shadow-offset); shadow opacity multiplies the shared age fade so it dies with its plane. Blur: pre-blurred SDF silhouettes (crisp icons have binary alpha — halo-blur needs a gradient) + altitude-driven icon-halo-width/blur deepening the penumbra. HONEST VERDICT at replay density (39 positioned), headless Chromium, both editions: day is a clear WIN — FL300+ shadows float a glyph-length off the page, low traffic keeps them tucked, Tampa cluster stays legible, no smudge; night READS but quieter — the night paper leaves little room to darken, shadows legible at lean-in over land and water (consistent with Q15c lean-in stance) though weaker than day; recommend keeping and letting the visual pass tune night alphas ([0 .55][40k .32] now). Screenshots: scratchpad shadow-day.png / shadow-night.png + crops. bb test 632+814 assertions green, bb lint clean. Same GeoJSON source, no React, no extra setData — 60fps unthreatened.

Shipped in 817b94f: #/preview renders the REAL chrome (header, NOTAM ribbon, index card fed ups-2717, legend, Stack excerpt with the 7700 tick) under five independent controls — typography (Marginalia/Drafting Room/Annotation), scale (1.333@13, 1.2@12, 1.25@12, 1.25@13), spacing (4px airy/8px strict/compact), palette (settled + wine-pen + plum-pen), manual day/night. Data-attributes on the page root swap CSS custom-property sets (the winner's ship mechanism); edition also drives adsb.map.theme. Mix persists in the URL hash + copyable readout; pasted links adopt live. OFL woff2s committed with LICENSE note; CSP font-src need noted on adsb-kh4.7 (no backend files touched). Suite green (168 browser tests/894 assertions incl. 9 new; lint clean) verified in an isolated worktree at HEAD because the shared tree was mid-churn from dgb.12; page also verified live in headless Chromium, both editions, mixed mixes. adsb-dgb.10 stays open for the Overseer's pick.

Perspective size implemented (commit 581befa): icon-size is the instinct-altitude channel — perspective-size-stops [[0 1.25][10000 1.0][40000 0.55]], front-loaded so the low sky keeps resolution; emergency 1.6 overrides absolutely; mlat demotion now a multiplicative 0.78 factor composed with the altitude size; ground 0.5 deliberately beneath the whole flying ramp (apron clutter defers — a size-test invariant caught the original 0.7 contradicting the declared intent, and the constant obeyed the intent). Cast shadow DELETED per the pick, not toggled: geo shadow-offset math+property, shadow inks/expressions/spec/soft-SDF variants, all shadow tests; preview rig removed (was never committed). Verified in the running app at replay density, both editions (scratchpad altitude-instinct/final-perspective-{day,night}.png): high cruisers recede, low traffic leans forward, night identical to day because geometry carries no ink. bb test 609+834 green, bb lint clean.

Overseer picked PERSPECTIVE SIZE from the four proofs (baseline/size/tether/size+tether; halo-weight disqualified on SDF physics); tether and shadow explicitly rejected by the Overseer. Evidence in scratchpad altitude-instinct/ + README; live ?preview= rig demonstrated all candidates in the running app and was removed after the pick. Winner implemented and shipped as adsb-b23 (commit 581befa) — shadow layer deleted with it, resolving adsb-dgb.8's remnant. Channel settled: colour = precision, size = instinct, Stack = profile.

Re-closed after Overseer-found caption defect fixed in 719645e (original ship 817b94f): the preview had generalized Drafting Room's --t-2 caption size to all three systems, rendering Marginalia's and The Annotation's captions a token smaller than their bake-off specs. Captions now default to --t-1 with B (and C's small pen headings) carrying their own --t-2 — each candidate judged as designed. Verified on the Overseer's own preferred mix (annotation/major-13/airy-4/wine-pen, both editions): fact labels compute 10.5px under Annotation@major-13, 8.5px caps under Drafting Room (its spec), 10px under Marginalia. CSS-only change; suite untouched and green from 817b94f. adsb-dgb.10 still open for the final pick.

PICKED by the Overseer 2026-07-12, live in #/preview (adsb-dgb.11): typography=annotation scale=major-13 spacing=compact-4 palette=wine-pen — The Annotation (Space Mono only, weight/case hierarchy, magenta pen), major third 1.25@13px (tokens 8.5/10.5/13/16/20), compact 4px spacing (2/4/8/12/16, header 36px), wine-pen accents (magenta #A83A63/#E77E9B, chrome aero #36547E/#8BA9D6). Written into docs/design-direction.md §5 (+ §2 accent rows re-inked with provenance note) in commit 03efeb7. Scale note: Overseer confirmed Annotation and compact explicitly; major-13 accepted via Auteur's advisory (1.333@13 was his other finalist — a one-token flip in §5 if he reverses). Applied to the real chrome by the visual pass adsb-dgb.5; CSP font-src via adsb-kh4.7. Basemap place-label voice deliberately left to dgb.5's eye.

Staged in commit (see git log): #/preview gains a Captions dimension — scribbled / printed-mono / printed-grotesk / printed-inter — orthogonal to typography, persisted in the URL like the rest. Space Grotesk 500 + Inter 600 OFL woff2s self-hosted with LICENSE notes. Verified live: all four voices compute correctly on the crowned mix (10.5px italic vs 8.5px caps) and the new faces load. Suite green (166 browser tests/871 assertions, lint clean). Overseer picks the caption voice; the pick amends design-direction §5's caption clause and dgb.5 applies it.

Cleared in the commit above: preview page/stylesheet/tests deleted, adsb.core reverted to the plain shell mount (stale #/preview links just show the chart — verified live), index.html link removed, audition-only fonts (Source Serif 4, IBM Plex Mono, Inter) and their LICENSE sections dropped; shipped faces (Space Mono, Space Grotesk) untouched. §5 mechanism note updated to point future re-skins at git history. kh4.7 CSP note refreshed (faces + @font-face now in app.css). Suite green after removal (155 tests/778 assertions, lint clean); app bundle 152->134 modules.

Shipped: §7 map annotations complete. Red-pen double ellipse (draws in once — iteration-count 1 provable inline — then holds), MAYDAY stamp (shared emergency-words copy, data-only 1 Hz updates, track-perpendicular placement), edge arrow (pure adsb.geo/edge-annotation for edge point + great-circle bearing + distance, chrome-aware pixel insets, click fires existing select — camera never hijacked). Seam grew bounds/on-move!. bb test 842 assertions green, lint clean; verified live both editions + off-screen, screenshots in session scratchpad emergency-annotations/. Commit 21ae3f0. NOTE: concurrent adsb.stream backoff-grows failure is in-flight backend WIP, not this bead.

Visual pass complete and signed off by the Overseer. Typography/spacing/color/motion cohere with docs/design-direction.md; phone and desktop both stanced; contrast and keyboard basics in; doc reconciled with the shipped code.

UI chrome epic complete: 12/12. Select, inspect, the Stack (no sidebar), header/legend/connection status, feeder status, emergency alerting in chrome AND on the chart, and the visual design pass — all shipped and signed off.

VERIFIED against the live deployment 2026-07-12 (live feeder, not replay). 20 s of 'curl -N https://map.bwawan.com/api/stream': one snapshot then an update every ~1.0 s, continuously — no buffering, no batching, no idle-timeout kill. The full chain is browser -> Cloudflare (bwawan.com zone) -> DigitalOcean's edge (itself Cloudflare) -> container, and SSE survives BOTH proxy layers unmodified. Same clean cadence straight at the origin. The feared managed-router buffering does not happen; the Caddy fallback recorded on this bead is not needed.

Overseer flipped the bwawan.com zone to Full (strict) 2026-07-12. Verified live: map.bwawan.com still serves (302 -> /index.html, the normal index redirect) and /api/stream opens 200 and holds. No 526, so Cloudflare validates the origin cert (Google Trust Services, *.ondigitalocean.app, chain OK). The feeder tunnel is unaffected as expected.

Done and verified. src/css (Garden) -> resources/public/app.css, gitignored build output. Ported all 127 rules; a rule-by-rule diff against the old hand-written stylesheet is IDENTICAL (same rules, order, declarations, declaration order), and a Playwright pixel diff of the rendered chrome in light+dark x desktop+phone is identical except the live clock's digits. bb css / bb css:watch; wired into bb dev, bb build, Dockerfile. Garden and org.flatland/ordered are :css/:test alias deps only - verified 0 garden classes in target/adsb.jar.

Landmine found and fixed: garden rebuilds declaration maps with (reduce assoc (empty m) m), and a >8-pair map literal is already a hash map at read time. That silently reordered declarations - .adsb-alert's 'border: none' landed AFTER 'border-bottom' (erasing the NOTAM row rules) and .adsb-edge-arrow's 'font: inherit' AFTER 'font-weight' (erasing its bold). Fixed with adsb.css.decl/decl (flatland ordered-map, survives garden's rebuild). adsb.css-test guards it and genuinely fails if decl is reverted.

VERIFIED against the live deployment 2026-07-13 after the origin token was set (CF Transform Rule stamps X-Origin-Token; ADSB_ORIGIN_TOKEN encrypted secret on DO).

  https://adsb-map-production-csszk.ondigitalocean.app/           -> 403 (was 200)
  ...same host /index.html, /api/stream                            -> 403
  ...same host /healthz                                            -> 200 (exempt, so DO's health check still passes — the container is not killed)
  https://map.bwawan.com/  -> 302, /healthz -> 200                  (real path unaffected)

The skip-Cloudflare door (the .ondigitalocean.app hostname) is shut. The container now answers only requests that carry the secret, which only our Cloudflare zone stamps.

CORRECTION to an earlier claim on this bead: the 'Host-spoof to a DO Cloudflare IP with Host: map.bwawan.com' I described as a SECOND open door was a misread. Re-tested with the lock on, that path is SERVED (302 / streaming), i.e. it carries the token — because Cloudflare routes by SNI: presenting SNI=map.bwawan.com to ANY Cloudflare edge IP is handled by the bwawan.com zone, Transform Rule and all. So it was never a bypass; it was the legitimate path reached via a different anycast IP. The only true bypass was the .ondigitalocean.app hostname (different zone, no Transform Rule), and that is what is now 403. DO fronts everything with Cloudflare and exposes no raw origin IP, so there is no non-Cloudflare path left to the container.

Phone: the shelves are chips (label + count), the dots stand down, and the ruler carries a 55% floor on its own axis in both stances. MEASURED at 390x844 against the running app: ruler 94px -> 253px live, and 234px even with both counts forced to 999 — the scale's length is now a property of the Stack, not of the shelves' population. Tapping a chip opens a sheet that NAMES its residents (34 rows, selectable, firing the same [:aircraft/select icao] contract) — the question the dot cluster could never answer, in either stance. Desktop keeps its dots; the count is hidden there, where the dots already are the count.

NOTE, beyond the bead as filed: the sheet is not phone-only. It is a popover above its chip and works in both stances, so the desktop gains tap-to-name too — additive, and only on a click. Desktop's default appearance is unchanged.

Browser tests: the chip counts its residents; the chip opens/closes the sheet; the dots and the sheet are never both in the DOM; a sheet row selects.

Hit-slop + scrub, both landed. The ruler's ticks get a transparent ::before at inset -9px — the ink stays 3px, the target does not — and the ruler itself is now scrubbable: press and drag, and axis-pct reads the pointer's place on the altitude axis while nearest-tick names and lights whatever it passes. Release fires [:aircraft/select icao].

It invents no view state: the scrub dispatches the EXISTING :aircraft/hover and :aircraft/select channels, so a scrubbed tick is named by the same code that names a hovered one, and the map will light it by the same key. The stance comes from the ruler's own rect (wider than tall = lying down), so one gesture serves both editions.

VERIFIED in the running app at 390x844 (Playwright, real pointer events): each press sets :aircraft/hovered-icao to the nearest tick, dragging renames it continuously as the finger passes ticks, and release selects it and opens the panel.

Browser tests: axis-pct in both stances, clamped, nil on a zero-area rect; nearest-tick; the shelves are unreachable by scrub (absent is not zero, and a finger cannot make it so); press-drag names; release selects; an UNPRESSED pointer does not scrub (the desktop's own hover keeps its rights); a cancelled gesture chooses nobody.

RESOLVED and verified live 2026-07-13. Root cause was config, not code: ADSB_TRUST_FORWARDED_FOR was unset on the deployed app, so client-ip fell through to the socket peer (DO's rotating internal 100.64/10 address) instead of CF-Connecting-IP. The diagnostic proved CF-Connecting-IP carries the real client (35.141.197.72, single/stable) — the double-Cloudflare topology does NOT mangle it. After setting ADSB_TRUST_FORWARDED_FOR=true and redeploying: 5 concurrent SSE from one IP through map.bwawan.com -> 4x200 + 1 refused (was 5x200). The client key no longer scatters; the cap binds on the real address.

One follow-on split to its own bead: the refusal currently surfaces as a Cloudflare 504 rather than the app's 503+Retry-After (adsb-<see new>). The scattering defect this bead tracked is fixed.

Done, and verified in the running app at 390px and 1440px — both stances fit with room to spare.

Stream chip: absent while live. Reconnecting/Disconnected keep everything they had.
Feeder: a bare 9px green dot at :ok, no box, label moved to the a11y tree via a new
.adsb-vh utility; starting/down/unknown keep their words in plain sight.
Stats: MAX RANGE and MSG RATE now read from the header beside the counts, unboxed; the
margin column is down to the legend alone.

Phone header now reads: adsb | 75 · 65 | MAX RANGE 256 km | MSG RATE 337/s | ●

Tests: a live stream renders no chip and no word; a healthy feeder's label is present in
the DOM but carries .adsb-vh; every problem state's label does NOT; the scalars read from
inside the header with their units. 881 assertions green (the 2 failures are adsb-kc4).

FOLLOW-UP WORTH A LOOK: the margin column's legend still prints an ALTITUDE colour ramp —
which is precisely what the Stack's ruler IS (design-direction §9: 'the ruler's fill is the
altitude gradient — it is its own legend'). That box is now the largest piece of chrome
covering the map, and half of it is a second copy of the ruler. Filing separately if wanted.

Done and verified in the running app at 390px and 1440px.

At rest the credit is now a 24x24 (i) button — 576px2, down from 8,453px2 (and 16,280px2 when it wrapped to two lines on a phone). One tap still opens the full 'OpenFreeMap © OpenMapTiles Data from OpenStreetMap'. The margin column dropped 62px with it (the 56px lift existed only to clear the wrapped banner), so the legend now sits beside the (i) instead of above it.

THE CATCH, for whoever touches this next: the fold must run on LOAD, not on create. At construction the control is still empty — the credit rides the style's sources — and MapLibre's compact logic skips an empty control, so the pass that OPENS the credit is the one that fires when the style lands. My first attempt folded it synchronously after create! and MapLibre re-opened it a moment later; the probe caught it (at rest: open=true, 352px wide). Moved to maplibre/on-load! and it sticks.

Tests: the control is configured and never switched off; compact is true; the fold drops the open-class while leaving maplibregl-compact (which is what stops MapLibre re-opening it AND what keeps the button drawn); and a container with no attribution control folds to nil rather than throwing — the failure mode leaves the credit visible, never missing.

Corrected and verified. The credit now SHOWS, then folds after five seconds — the timeout the OSMF guidelines name by number — with MapLibre's own collapse-on-pan/zoom still firing first if the reader touches the map. Both are permitted triggers; the (i) is reachable forever after either way.

attribution-fold-ms is a licence term wearing a number's clothes. Its docstring and its test both say so: it may not go to zero, and it may not be shortened. If someone later 'optimises' it to fold on load, they will break the licence, and the test will stop them.

Verified in the running app at 390px: banner shown at t=2.5s (352x24), folded at t=6.5s (24x24), and folded at t=2.4s when panned early. The margin column still reclaims its 56px of map — it just waits for the fold, stepping down when the map container gains the .adsb-credit-folded mark.

LESSON, recorded because it nearly shipped: I asserted 'the credit is one tap away, so this is fine' without reading the guidelines. The guidelines were one WebFetch away and they disagreed. A compaction that touches a REQUIRED attribution is a licensing question first and a design question second.

Legend deleted; the Stack is the key. Verified in the running app at 390px and 1440px: no .adsb-legend, no .adsb-margin, and the swatches render the palette exactly (GND #8A8374, NO ALT #9A937F — the day edition's own ground/unknown inks, read back off data-color and matching the painted background).

The margin column is gone entirely — component, stylesheet namespace, and the div. The bottom-left corner is map again. app.css: 21,764 -> 20,706 bytes.

THE ONE THING THAT NEEDED CARE, recorded for whoever revisits this: the legend's value was not the box, it was that it built its swatches from style/palette directly, so the key could not drift from the planes across a re-skin. Moving the key onto the Stack as CSS tokens would have silently killed that. The swatches are painted inline from the palette instead, and legend_test's both-editions equality assertion is ported onto them (the-key-cannot-drift-from-the-map-in-either-edition), including the not= guard proving the two editions really are different inks so the test is a live constraint and not a tautology.

docs/design-direction.md §11 amended: the margin column is recorded as deleted, with the three moves that emptied it (adsb-33i stats to the header, adsb-sod key to the Stack, adsb-5c7 the credit folded).

884 assertions green (the 2 reds are adsb-kc4).

Fixed 2026-07-13: test:clj now :depends [css], so bb test (locally on a fresh clone and in CI, which runs bb test) compiles the Garden stylesheet before the JVM suite. Matches the build task's existing :depends [css] — one source of truth, no ci.yml change. Verified by deleting resources/public/app.css and running bb test:clj: it wrote app.css (20,581 bytes) then passed 154 tests / 664 assertions. NOTE: CI will still be red until adsb-kc4 (deterministic backoff-grows failure in the cljs browser suite) is fixed — that is a separate defect, not the CSS ordering.
Fixed in 9572326: reg-fx now wraps schedule-reconnect! in a lambda so the test's redef seam actually works. backoff-grows passes; full cljs suite green (182 tests, 898 assertions).

Fixed: setPointerCapture in on-ruler-down!, scrub-end dispatches clear-hover after select, native pointerleave listener on .adsb-stack clears unconditionally. Reproduced the wedge live before (drag off ruler, release; hover stuck across the session), verified gone after. Browser tests: a-released-scrub-puts-the-crosshair-down, leaving-the-stack-clears-any-hover-unconditionally. Commit ce3ff39.

Counts print in both stances; chip wraps (width 100%, flex-wrap) with count justified to the shelf edge; phone re-flows to nowrap captions; PLOTTED sheds its placeholder swatch per its own docstring. Verified live at desktop width (GND 0 / NO ALT 7 / EMG 0 / PLOTTED 27/29 all legible in the 76px column) and at 390px in headless Chromium. CSS test pins never-hidden. Commit ce3ff39.

New adsb.css.card holds the shared face + close decls; panel and drawer compose it. Verified both stances visually. CSS test pins card-on-desktop/sheet-on-phone. Commit ce3ff39.

ZOOM census chip while zoom > 1 (states x-ratio, resets on press), crosshair cursor on the ruler view. Verified live on desktop and at 390px headless: chip joins the caption line, reset restores full window with zero overflow markers. Commit ce3ff39.

reduced-motion emitted last via app.clj (motion/styles now keyframes-only); drawer added to the animation:none group. css_test reduced-motion-wins-every-tie pins the order against animation: adsb-settle/breathe/ring-draw and transition: bottom. Commit ce3ff39.

Phone drawer is a bottom sheet now: bottom calc(--stack-w + safe-inset), 30vh cap, borders on the two on-screen edges, adsb-settle-up keyframes (rises out of the bar). Panel keeps the top; verified no overlap at 390x844 with both open. css_test pins the anchoring. Commit b64484f.

Column composition: --stack-w 96px standing (phone re-pins 76px via :root override in adsb.css.phone — the bar spends the token as height); one-line census chips with counts justified to the edge (PLOTTED wraps to a right-justified stat line only when the fraction outgrows the column); ruler 26px wide, ticks slimmed to 2px (hover/selected 4, emergency 6) so the day ramp reads as the map key; margin under the ruler clears SFC. Verified day and night, 1440 and 390. Commit b64484f.
